prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Arrr, gather 'round ye scallywags and lend me yer ears, for Cap'n will tell ye of this great mystery!

Ye see, when the great blazin' sun sends its light down upon Davy Jones' world, that light be carryin' ALL the colors of the rainbow within it - aye, every last one, like treasure hidden in a single chest!

Now, the sky above be filled with tiny, TINY bits of air - smaller than the smallest grain o' sand on any beach I've plundered. And when that sunlight comes crashin' into all them tiny bits, they scatter the light about like cannonballs bouncin' off the hull of a ship!

HERE be the secret, ye bilge rats - the BLUE light, it scatters the MOST! It flies and bounces in every direction across the whole of the heavens, whilst the other colors, the reds and the yellows, they pass right on through with less fuss, like a merchant ship too cowardly to fight!

So when ye look up with yer one good eye - or both, if ye be fortunate enough to still have the pair - ye see blue scattered across the whole sky in every direction!

The same trickery be why sunsets run RED and ORANGE, when the light travels longer through the air to reach yer weathered face!

Now BACK TO THE OARS!
